entry.title: 2025 ASUG Best Practices for Oil, Gas, and Energy
entry.content:
Snapshot of dynamic leaders engaged in keynote/panel discussion at last year's ASUG Best Practices 

Goodness is coming! 

Our 2025 ASUG Best Practices for Oil, Gas, and Energy is set for October 6-8th, 2025.  For 2025, the event returns to its singular focus on the Oil, Gas, & Energy industry, no longer co-located and combined with other industries as was the offering of this event in 2024. Additionally for 2025, we return to the hospitable city of Houston, Texas, where the Hilton Americas-Houston will serve as the venue for the 2025 ASUG Best Practices for Oil, Gas, and Energy event.

Proceed now with informing and inviting your customers to join us in Houston for this premier gathering of customers, partners, and professionals within the SAP ecosystem.  The event will enable all to connect with peers, learn from industry experts, and discover how to drive transformative results in their organization.  The event kicks-off with an empowering and enlightening keynote from ExxonMobil’s, Kurt Aerts, Director, Plastics and Product Life Cycle.   The keynote from Kurt Aerts will not only inform and inspire, but it will set the stage for what we anticipate will be an event and overall conference experienced saturated with goodness.  In addition to the opening keynote, Day 1 will provide a welcoming reception that is sure to give all the opportunity to connect with industry leaders and fellow attendees. Day 2 continues with the goodness, featuring keynotes, customer journeys, deep dive sessions, and an insider's look into SAP roadmaps.   Day 3, being the final day of the event will cap off the experience with expert sessions, dynamic discussions, and hands-on learning opportunities.  Throughout the event exciting customer stories, partner sessions, and talks by experts will be shared, equipping participants with the knowledge and tools to:

  • Optimize Asset Management and Lifecycle Strategies: Discover SAP solutions to maximize the value of your investments and extend asset lifecycles, ensuring operational efficiency across various industries.
  • Enhance Operational Agility and Resilience: Learn how to implement best practices for streamlined operations, enabling your organization to adapt quickly to market fluctuations and disruptions.
  •  Strengthen Data Security and Governance: Explore SAP's capabilities for robust data security practices, compliance, and effective data governance strategies.
  • Optimize Supply Chain Efficiency: Gain insights into digital supply chain management strategies that enhance efficiency and resilience and are adaptable to a wide range of industries.
  • Empower Your Workforce with Advanced SAP Technologies: Master the latest SAP tools and techniques to improve user experience, drive workforce productivity, and foster innovation.

The customer line-up that will grace the stage at the event telling their amazing stories of transformation and SAP's role in enabling and facilitating that transformation include, just to list a few, the following:  ExxonMobil, BPx Energy, Chevron, Precision Drilling, Merit Energy, CITGO Petroleum, Southwest Gas, Oxy, Messer.

Indeed, goodness is coming, so get ready and plan to be present at what will be an enriching and enjoyable event of pure SAP Oil, Gas, & Energy goodness!  Access the LIVE Agenda!  Register Now!

We look forward to seeing and connecting with you in Houston, Texas, October 6 - 8, 2025 for the 2025 ASUG Best Practices for Oil, Gas, and Energy conference that is certainly poised to position organizations to be their best.

entry.title: Re: Error "???COMMON_Survey_Invitation_Content???"
entry.content:
Hello  @Connie3,

I can confirm that the survey pop-up shown is not related to LMS surveys.

Instead, this pop-up is part of our initiative to gather product feedback from users. We periodically prompt users to take a product feedback survey on specific pages about 3-5 times a year. Additionally, on all pages, you can launch the survey using the icon located in the page header. Help document link: Product Feedback | SAP Help Portal

Please note that the survey pop-up which is triggered on a random basis cannot be turned off.

I would request you to submit a case with us under component LOD-SF-LMS to investigate this further because BizX labels are populated instead of the actual text in the pop-up.

This has been reported by multiple customers and we are currently working on the fix.

 If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Swapnil

entry.title: Unable to access Manage Service Partner Users
entry.content:
S-user ID(s) of the admins receiving the error:

S-user ID of the user they are trying to manage:

Steps to reproduce the issue:

  1. In SAP for Me open Manage Service Partner Users
  2. Get error message: 'You do not have sufficient authorizations to access this application. In order to continue, a Cloud administrator role is required. To receive this role, please send a request to your company's Super or Cloud Administrator.'

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access Manage Service Partner Users
entry.content:
To access Manage Service Partner Users , S-user ID from end customer number who is providing authorizations must be listed as a Cloud Administrator in Important Contacts area. The provided S-user has not yet been designated as a Cloud Administrator, which is why the user is unable to access the Manage Service Partner Users feature and is encountering the specified error.

To enable the S-user to access Manage Service Partner Users, please have the existing Cloud Administrator grant the two authorizations listed below, along with any relevant cloud authorizations, to the S-user ID (refer to KBA 2198153).Tip: For more information on the role of cloud admin authorizations for users, please see KBA 2916760. 

  • Edit User Data 
  • Edit Authorizations

Once S-user has been granted the Cloud Administrator role, they will be able to access Manage Service Partner Users and grant the necessary authorizations to partners without any issues.

For more detailed information, please refer to the KBAs below: 

2596214 - How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me
2518600 - How does a customer invite partner S-user IDs to create cases on their behalf using the Service Partner User (SPU) functionality - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Verification field on RF screen is not editable
entry.content: Verification is used when it is something to verify, if product is empty (System does not know which product you found) you have nothing to verify, and you input product in the input field. It should accept barcode as well (usually it is a conversion exit which would determine Product by barcode). And if not you can do it with a development.

entry.title: Re: Automating Rate Changes in SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
By the way, you can also combine Job Posting Templates that leverage Rate Change Matrix under 'Rates 1.0' and Job Posting Templates that use our new 'Rates 2.0' setup by enabling  'Co-existence' of the two rate models.

So say e.g. you want to use buyer defined Rate Change Matrix in Germany to automate 'Branchenzuschläge' but want to use Rates 2.0 and its lean and simple setup for your UK operations, that can be done when co-existence is enabled in your tenant.

entry.title: Health Monitoring in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Efficient Monitoring in SAP Datasphere: Why You Need More Than the Standard Tools

SAP Datasphere allows users to quickly and intuitively develop even complex data models. That’s one of the key reasons why many customers rely on it for larger-scale self-service analytics. However, as usage increases, it becomes critically important to manage available resources efficiently and to identify problematic data models, queries, and SAC stories early.

For effective platform operations in SAP Datasphere, ongoing monitoring of resource consumption and continuous optimization of developments are essential. In numerous customer conversations, we’ve seen how a lack of appropriate monitoring can lead to severe system issues—slow performance, failing queries, or error messages like “Database is busy”.

In this blog post, I’ll address the following key questions:

  • What metrics are most important for monitoring SAP Datasphere?

  • What standard monitoring capabilities does SAP Datasphere offer?

  • What challenges do users face when relying solely on the standard tools?

  • What is the ISR Monitoring Solution?

  • What advantages does the ISR solution offer for efficient and proactive monitoring?


Which Key Metrics Matter in SAP Datasphere Monitoring?

Effective monitoring of SAP Datasphere relies on tracking the right performance indicators. Among the most important KPIs are:

  • CPU utilization

  • Memory usage

  • Number of active threads

These are highlighted in blue on the left side of Figure above.

What influences these KPIs?

On the right side of the diagram, you'll find the key drivers that directly impact system performance, including:

  • Expensive Statements: Queries that consume above-average system resources.

  • MDS Statements from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC): Queries sent from SAC to SAP Datasphere.

  • Executed Tasks: Scheduled tasks and jobs within the system.

Additional critical events to monitor:

  • Admission Control Events: These occur when queries are delayed or rejected due to high system load.

  • Out of Memory Events: Situations where the system lacks sufficient memory to execute a query or task.

Monitoring these KPIs and events provides a solid foundation for identifying performance bottlenecks early and maintaining a stable SAP Datasphere environment.

What Standard Monitoring Capabilities Does SAP Datasphere Offer?

SAP Datasphere provides several standard tools and methods to monitor system health and performance. However, these often require manual effort and custom SQL to get meaningful insights.

How Can Key Metrics Be Measured?

  • CPU, Memory, and Threads
    These metrics can be monitored via the SAP HANA Cockpit or by using a DB analysis user to execute custom SQL queries on the tables:

    • M_LOAD_HISTORY_HOST

    • HOST_LOAD_HISTORY_HOST

  • Expensive Statements
    These can be identified by querying the table:

    • M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS
      This also requires custom SQL code and a DB analysis user.

  • MDS Statements (from SAP Analytics Cloud)
    These are only partially visible through the standard SAP Datasphere System Monitoring. For more detailed analysis, the following tables can be queried:

    • M_MULTIDIMENSIONAL_STATEMENTS

    • M_MULTIDIMENSIONAL_STATEMENT_STATISTICS

  • Task Management
    Monitoring of tasks is available per space through the Integration Monitor. For a cross-space overview, you’ll need to run custom SQL queries (via a DB analysis user) on the:

    • TASK_LOGS 

While these standard tools and tables offer access to relevant data, they often require advanced knowledge and manual query work. In practice, this limits their usefulness for proactive, scalable monitoring. Beside the DB Analysis User Option some tables could also be integrated in a Datasphere Space by activation of monitoring views in the Configuration.
(Cloud ALM is not considered here)

What Are the Challenges of Monitoring with Standard SAP Tools?

The standard tools provided by SAP Datasphere offer only limited monitoring capabilities. First and foremost, there is no single integrated monitoring solution. Instead, administrators must navigate multiple entry points such as:

  • System Monitoring

  • Integration Monitoring

  • SAP HANA Cockpit

This fragmented approach means system admins constantly switch between tools to gain a full picture.

Limited Functionality and Lack of Root Cause Analysis

Even when using all available tools, the built-in features provide limited functionality. For example, it's very difficult—if not impossible—to conduct a root cause analysis like:

"What caused the high CPU load this morning?"

Critical components such as Expensive Statements are missing or incomplete. Performing meaningful analysis often requires custom SQL code, which typically must be executed by a DB analysis user. Moreover, key data points (like Expensive Statements) are not historically logged, making it difficult to trace performance issues over time and forcing users to build additional custom solutions.

High Technical Overhead

A deep level of technical expertise is required to identify and optimize resource-intensive processes. Monitoring often feels like assembling a complex puzzle from scratch every time—costly, time-consuming, and prone to error.

The Need for an Advanced Monitoring Solution

To monitor SAP Datasphere effectively, custom extensions are almost always necessary, as the standard tools fall short of delivering the full picture. However, this approach increases both workload and risk. There is a clear need for advanced, automated monitoring solutions that provide comprehensive, efficient, and proactive system oversight.

Health Monitoring by ISR Information Products AG for SAP Datasphere

At ISR Information Products AG, we have developed an integrated and automated monitoring solution tailored specifically for SAP Datasphere.

At the heart of this solution is the Health Monitoring Cockpit—a central dashboard built in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). It visualizes the most important KPIs in real time and enables in-depth analysis of system performance and resource usage.

This cockpit provides a comprehensive, user-friendly overview of your Datasphere environment and helps both platform teams and developers quickly identify and resolve performance issues.

Centralized Dashboard with Integrated KPI Analysis

Our Health Monitoring solution offers a comprehensive dashboard that brings together all critical monitoring KPIs in one place. Built on a standardized data model, it correlates key metrics and influencing factors, enabling users to recognize trends and perform root cause analysis efficiently.

Key Features at a Glance:

  • User-friendly dashboard
    Intuitive interface for both technical and business users, no SQL knowledge required.

  • Integrated KPI analysis
    All relevant metrics—CPU, memory, threads, task load, query performance—presented in one consolidated view.

  • Deep-dive capabilities
    Drill-down into statement-level details, including expensive queries and SAC-driven activity, to uncover root causes of performance issues.

This approach allows platform teams to shift from reactive monitoring to proactive performance management, significantly reducing time spent on troubleshooting.

Identifying Root Causes

 

One of the key strengths of the ISR Health Monitoring solution is its ability to visually correlate system KPIs with their underlying drivers. For example, a spike in CPU usage can be analyzed in direct relation to influencing factors such as MDS statements triggered by SAP Analytics Cloud.

Root Cause Analysis – Made Visual and Actionable

  • Visual correlation between key KPIs (e.g., high CPU load) and potential causes (e.g., specific SAC queries or data models).

  • Drill-down analysis using the SAP Data Analyzer to pinpoint the root cause—such as a specific story execution—that led to the performance issue.

This capability turns raw system data into actionable insights, helping platform teams and developers address performance issues before they impact end users.

Trend Analysis

Stay on top of how your SAP Datasphere environment evolves over time. Our monitoring solution enables you to identify long-term trends, such as increasing memory consumption, recurring query spikes, or system load patterns.

You remain in full control of the data history—flexibly define how long historical data should be retained in the model to suit your operational and compliance needs.

Benefits of Trend Analysis:

  • Spot performance degradation early

  • Detect inefficient developments or increasing resource demands

  • Support capacity planning and proactive optimization

With this, trend monitoring becomes a key part of your platform strategy—not just reactive, but forward-looking.

Task Management

 

Gain a comprehensive overview of all running tasks within your SAP Datasphere system. Our solution makes it easy to identify the most resource-intensive tasks based on memory and CPU consumption.

Thanks to intuitive navigation, you can access detailed task-level insights with just a few clicks—supporting efficient, targeted performance monitoring.

Key Benefits:

  • Full visibility into scheduled and active tasks

  • Quick identification of performance bottlenecks

  • Drill-down analysis for deeper optimization

This ensures your operations team can act quickly and precisely—keeping your system stable and responsive.

Spot Resource Hogs – Identify Expensive Objects Instantly

 

Our monitoring solution automatically analyzes your SAP Datasphere environment and highlights the most resource-intensive objects—at a glance.

This allows you to quickly see which objects require optimization before they lead to high memory or CPU usage.

Why It Matters:

  • Prevent performance issues before they impact users

  • Focus on the true cost drivers in your system

  • Optimize proactively rather than reactively

It’s a powerful tool for ensuring your system runs smoothly—even as complexity and usage grow.

What Advantages Do ISR Solution Offer?

Our monitoring solution is an integrated, automated system that delivers many benefits:

Significant Reduction of Complexity

Our monitoring data model includes extensive SQL logic—such as parsing JSON-based MDS statements and linking them with CPU utilization—and standardizations like unifying CPU metrics. These features greatly simplify the complexity for users of our data model and cockpit, abstracting away technical details to provide clear, actionable insights.

Reduction of Manual Effort

Comprehensive monitoring of SAP Datasphere typically requires significant manual effort—switching between multiple UI screens (e.g., HANA Cockpit) and running custom SQL scripts for analysis. The ISR monitoring solution automates many manual tasks, such as searching for resource hogs, drastically reducing time and effort.

Centralized Monitoring Cockpit

Our solution provides a central cockpit for SAP Datasphere monitoring, summarizing all key information in one place. Access is controlled by space-level authorizations, enabling decentralized use—for example, by different business units within a self-service architecture.

Once resource-intensive objects are identified, users can perform deep-dive analyses down to individual SQL statements using the SAP Analytics Cloud Data Analyzer.

Integrated View of KPIs and Influencing Factors

The solution links key platform KPIs (such as CPU utilization) with their drivers (like executed tasks, MDS/SAC requests, and expensive statements). This connection enables rapid identification of resource-heavy developments and provides guidance for ongoing optimization of data models and SAC stories.

If you have further questions, please contact me (e.g. via LinkedIn). You want to see the solution in action -> See a webinar replay (only in german)

entry.title: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content:
Hi, I'm developing a SAPUI5 application and I need to instantiate a fragment (F) within a view (V).

The issue is that the view (V) can be closed by the user. To ensure good performance, I simply destroy it using:

V.destroyDependents();
V.destroyContent();
V.destroy();

However, the user can reopen the same view, which means I need to re-instantiate it, and with it, the fragment. I'm using this code:

Fragment.load({
    name: "webapp.fragment.DocumentaleDialog",
    id: this.createId("dialogDocumentale"),
    controller: this
}).then((oFragment) => {
    that.oDocumentaleDialog = oFragment;
    that.oView.addDependent(that.oDocumentaleDialog);
    openDocumentaleDialog();
});

The problem I'm encountering is a duplicate ID error when I try to instantiate the fragment again.

entry.title: Connecting S/4HANA On-Premise to SAP Advanced Event Mesh: A Matter of Trust!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

When connecting S/4HANA On-Premise to SAP Advanced Event Mesh, many of the configuration steps are well-documented. This blog post is intended to complement the primary setup blog. We will take a deep dive into what is often the most challenging part of the process: establishing mutual trust between AEM and S/4HANA through proper certificate management.

This connection is a crucial step towards building a robust, event-driven architecture. It allows your core business processes to publish and consume events, enabling real-time responsiveness and decoupled integrations. This guide provides a straightforward, step-by-step approach to get your S/4HANA system and AEM communicating, with a special focus on the certificate handling.

Prerequisites

Before we begin, ensure you have the following:

  • Access to your S/4HANA On-Premise system with authorization for transactions STRUST, SMICM, and /IWXBE/CONFIG.
  • An active SAP Advanced Event Mesh instance running in your SAP BTP subaccount.
  • The AEM Validation service must be active in the same BTP subaccount where your AEM instance resides. This is key for the setup.

Part 1: Preparing S/4HANA Certificates in STRUST

The first step is to establish a proper identity for your S/4HANA system by replacing the default dummy certificates. All of this is done in transaction STRUST.

  1. Navigate to STRUST: Open the transaction STRUST. You will see several PSE (Personal Security Environment) nodes on the left. We will primarily focus on "SSL Server Standard" and "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)".

     
     

  2. Load CA Root Certificates: For each PSE, you need to load the necessary CA root and intermediate certificates. You can obtain the necessary SAP Root CA certificates from your corporate certificate authority. Add them to the certificate least one by one.

  3. Create a Certificate Signing Request (CSR):

    • Right-click on a node (e.g. in our case, "SSL Server Standard") and choose "Replace". 
    • Key in the details of your server e.g. CN=mys4.mydomain.com and so on.
    • Save the PSE for your client.
    • Click the "Create Certificate Request" button.
    • Copy the generated CSR text.

  4. Sign the CSR: Submit this CSR to your corporate Certificate Authority to receive a signed certificate response.

  5. Import the Certificate Response:

    • Back in STRUST, with the corresponding node selected, click the "Import Certificate Response" button.
    • Paste the response you received from the CA.
    • You should see the chain of certificates similar to that image below.

  6. Repeat for the SSL Client: Repeat steps 3-5 for the "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)" PSE. This client certificate is crucial as it will be used to authenticate S/4HANA against Advanced Event Mesh. Note: The screenshots just show the client, it's the same for the server.

  7. Save: It's good practice to save the certificates to the database certificate store for persistence.

Part 2: Establishing Two-Way Trust

For a successful connection, S/4HANA must trust AEM, and AEM must trust S/4HANA. This is achieved by exchanging certificates.

Step 2.1: AEM Trusts S/4HANA

We need to provide AEM with the public key of the S/4HANA client certificate we configured in Part 1.

  1. Export S/4HANA Client Certificates:

    • In STRUST, navigate to the "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)" node.
    • You should see your certificate chain (Your Client Cert -> Intermediate CA -> Root CA).
    • Export each of these three certificates one by one in Base64 (PEM) format.

  2. Import into AEM:

    • Log in to your AEM Cluster Manager.
    • Navigate to your Broker, click Manage, and go to the Authentication and Security section.
    • Select the Certificate Authorities tab.
    • Upload the three certificate files you exported from S/4HANA. You should have three new entries when you are done.

Step 2.2: S/4HANA Trusts AEM

Now, we must configure S/4HANA to trust the certificates presented by the AEM broker endpoint.

  1. Get the AEM Broker Certificates:

    • The easiest way to do this is by using your web browser. Navigate to your AEM Broker Manager URL (https://*.messaging.solace.cloud).
    • Click the padlock icon in the browser's address bar and view the certificate chain.
    • Export the entire chain. Typically, this includes a root CA (e.g., "DigiCert Global Root G2"), an intermediate CA (e.g., "Thawte TLS RSA CA G1"), and the server certificate itself (e.g., *.messaging.solace.cloud).
    • Note: Your specific CA names may differ.
    • You can see int the screenshot below that by navigating up and down you can export all 3 certificates (here we just need the lower 2).

  2. Import into S/4HANA:

    • In STRUST, navigate to the "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)" node.
    • Import the AEM root and intermediate CA certificates you just downloaded into the certificate list. This ensures any certificate signed by this CA will be trusted.

Part 3: Configuring the Connection

With trust established, we can now configure the connection itself.

  1. Enable Client Certificate Auth in AEM:

    • Inside your AEM Broker Manager, navigate to Messaging -> Access Control -> Client Authentication.
    • Enable the "Client Certificate Authentication" option.

     

  2. Create a Client Username in AEM:

    • Still in the Broker Manager, create a new Client Username.
    • Crucially, the name must exactly match the Common Name (CN) of your S/4HANA "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)" certificate from STRUST.

     

  3. Create the Channel in S/4HANA:

    • Go to transaction /IWXBE/CONFIG.
    • Click the "Create new channel via Service Key" button.
    • In the drop-down menu, select "SAP Advanced Event Mesh".

  4. Use the Service Key:

    • In your BTP subaccount, find the service instance for the AEM Validation service.
    • Create a service key and copy the JSON content.
    • Paste this service key into the dialog in S/4HANA.

    The system will use the service key to automatically configure the necessary RFC destination and connection details. Perform a connection test to validate!

Troubleshooting: The Dreaded SSL Error

The most common issue is the SSL certificate not trusted (ICM_HTTP_SSL_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED) error during a connection test. Here’s how to pinpoint the problem:

  1. Activate ICM Trace:

    • Go to transaction SMICM.
    • From the menu, choose Goto -> Trace -> Reset -> All Files. This gives you a clean slate.
    • Set the trace level to 2 or 3 and check the box for "Trace encrypted data".

  2. Reproduce the Error: Go back to /IWXBE/CONFIG and run the connection test again to trigger the error.

  3. Analyze the Trace:

    • In SMICM, go to Goto -> Trace -> Display File.
    • Search for *** ERROR. You will find a section detailing the validation failure.

    Here is a real-world example from a trace file:

    BEGIN VERIFICATION RESULT
#   Messages
ERROR: The chain of certificates is incomplete or untrusted, missing certificate of [C8:02:5F:9F] CN=DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA2
#   Summary
#01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID
 Subject:           CN=*.cf.jp10.hana.ondemand.com, O=SAP SE...
 Issuer:            CN=DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US
 PKI validation:    FAILED: Validation of dependents - Issuer Certificate (Issuer - Only Invalid Certificates Found)
...
cli SSL session PSE "/usr/sap/S4H/D00/sec/SAPSSLC.pse"
    • Interpretation: The ERROR: line tells you exactly which certificate is missing (CN=DigiCert Global G2...). The line
      cli SSL session PSE, confirms that the system is using the Client PSE (SAPSSLC.pse), so you know you need to upload the missing certificate into the "SSL Client SSL Client (Standard)" node in STRUST.

Conclusion

By carefully setting up the certificates to establish mutual trust and then using the service key for automatic configuration, you can create a stable and secure connection between S/4HANA On-Premise and SAP Advanced Event Mesh. This unlocks powerful capabilities for your enterprise.

I hope this detailed walkthrough helps you in your integration journey. Feel free to leave questions or share your own experiences in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Can't send documents using the Microsoft Outlook
entry.content:
Hi kumu94 

In relation to further investigation of this reported Outlook instance issue, could you please check and make sure that Outlook is set as the default email program on the Operating system and please make sure that programmatic access security is not stopping Business One from accessing the application (trust center).

Also please check and confirm what Office version you are using? Usually when something like this happens, its either a permission issue (mismatch 32/64bit.), or Business One cannot find the Office installation. Also this could happen for instance when Office on the machine has been upgraded and there are still traces from the old installation.

 

Most of the cases it would be the Office installation. If anything, it would be worth to do a clean installation of Office on one of the machines and then check again.

Kind Regards
Nidhi 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP ChaRM TOC
entry.content:
Hi,

probably you are looking at the wrong tool for the new setup. Feeding additional clients on the same development system is better done with SCC1N.

The idea of ToCs is to test in the consolidation system keeping the import queues of the delivery system(s) clean, please see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/48db938dceba518de10000000a42189c.html?version=7.2.15&locale=en-US 

If you skip the ToC for the consolidation system, you have also to adjust the ChaRM check framework, where a check in status E0009 controls that the real TR is not newer than the ToC before releasing this real TR.

entry.title: Re: Material Consumption Report- Movement types
entry.content:
Hello @SB1184 

Sorry, but that is wrong. Debit/Credit indicator (the field SHKZG) = H, tells you that a movement type reduces stock, not that it is a consumption. Not every stock reduction is consumption! For instance, the movement type 102 reduces stock as it is reversal of purchase order receipt. But it is definitely not consumption.

Consumption movement types are set up as such in OMMJ configuration:

That is the T156-KZVBU field. You need to use that field.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Como extrair o melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e personalizações Clean Core.
entry.content:
Palestrante: Lucas Ferreira
Biografia: Arquiteto de soluções SAP com grande experiência em SAP Fiori, Clean Core e implementações Greenfield. Atua em grandes projetos de transformação digital com foco em boas práticas e inovação sustentável.
Título da palestra: Como extrair o melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e personalizações Clean Core
Descrição breve: A palestra explora como extrair valor da plataforma SAP utilizando o SAP Fiori e mantendo o princípio do Clean Core, essencial para manter a flexibilidade e atualização constante em ambientes SAP modernos. Através de exemplos práticos e cases reais, Lucas detalha as possibilidades de personalização sem comprometer a integridade do sistema.

Resumo

  • Cenário realista de transformação Greenfield: Lucas descreve a migração de plantas do ECC para o S/4HANA em ambiente Greenfield, destacando a redução de relatórios de manufatura de 111 para apenas 13 com SAP Fiori, mostrando como a padronização pode manter eficiência sem complexidade.

  • Escolha estratégica de ferramentas analíticas: Com base na realidade de sua empresa, Lucas justifica o uso do Power BI e MicroStrategy no lugar do SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) quando o objetivo é apenas BI, ressaltando o alto custo do SAC. Por outro lado, para planejamento, ele enaltece o SAC como a melhor ferramenta disponível.

  • Integração inteligente para decisões estratégicas: Um case com SAC permitiu priorizar ordens de manutenção numa empresa com 89 unidades, gerando um ganho de US$ 236 milhões. Isso ilustra como análises avançadas com SAC aplicadas à operação podem impactar diretamente o resultado financeiro.

  • Discussão sobre namespace e Clean Core: Lucas debate se soluções com namespace próprio (como as de parceiros) ainda podem ser consideradas Clean Core. Conclui que, desde que não afetem atualizações e migração, são válidas dentro dessa filosofia.

  • Desafios e lacunas no Clean Core: Casos como a nota fiscal eletrônica (NFE) exigem uso de técnicas ABAP tradicionais, que ainda não possuem uma alternativa Clean Core viável. A mensagem é clara: 100% Clean Core é uma meta, mas não uma realidade absoluta.

  • Capacitação e mercado de consultoria: É destacado que muitas consultorias ainda não dominam o Clean Core, o que limita a adoção plena pelos clientes. Para suprir essa carência, Lucas aponta a importância da formação e atualização contínua dos profissionais.

  • Exemplo prático de inovação com integração via maquininha POS: Lucas apresenta uma solução embarcada em maquininhas Android (Smart POS) integradas ao SAP Public Cloud. Com BTP e ABAP Cloud, automatiza o recebimento com cartão e a baixa contábil em tempo real, melhorando experiência do usuário e reduzindo erros operacionais.

  • Exploração do SAP Public Edition: A palestra conclui com uma análise sobre o SAP Public Edition e os desafios para personalizações, mostrando como APIs públicas e o uso do BTP permitem inovação mesmo em ambientes restritos por Clean Core.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 navigation tab
entry.content:
hi ,

for you requirement, wizard is the best option. 
However to implement it with Icon Tab Bar, below code worked. I know it is too late and you might not want a solution now. this could help others.
Add these two buttons (Prev and Next) in each of your icon tab filters in View file. Also, make all icon tab filters disabled except first one. ( you can handle them from controller as well based on some parameters or model )

<IconTabFilter text="People" key="people" enabled="false">
    <Text text="People content goes here ..." />
    <Button text="Prev" press="onPrevPress"></Button>                
    <Button text="Next" press="onNextPress"></Button>
</IconTabFilter>

Add below two methods in your controller.
Note : event - source - parent points to the current Icon Tab filter and its parent points to the icon tab bar. There is no method called setSelectedIndex so below is a work around

// trial
onNextPress: function(oEvent){
  const oSource = oEvent.getSource().getParent(); // current icon tab filter
  const oParent = oSource.getParent(); // icon tab bar
  const aItems = oParent.getItems(); // all items of the icon tab bar
  const iCP = oParent.indexOfItem(oSource); // current index

  // disable all
  aItems.forEach((oItem)=>{
    oItem.setEnabled(false);
  });

  // enable next and set it as selected
  iCP === aItems.length - 1 
  ? aItems[0].setEnabled(true) & oParent.setSelectedKey(aItems[0].getKey())
  : aItems[iCP + 1].setEnabled(true) & oParent.setSelectedKey(aItems[iCP + 1].getKey());
},
onPrevPress : function(oEvent){
  const oSource = oEvent.getSource().getParent(); // current icon tab filter
  const oParent = oSource.getParent(); // icon tab bar
  const aItems = oParent.getItems(); // all items of the icon tab bar
  const iCP = oParent.indexOfItem(oSource); // current index
  
  // disable all
  aItems.forEach((oItem)=>{
    oItem.setEnabled(false);
  });

  // enable previous and set it as selected 
  iCP === 0 
  ? aItems[aItems.length - 1].setEnabled(true) & oParent.setSelectedKey(aItems[aItems.length - 1].getKey())
  : aItems[iCP - 1].setEnabled(true) & oParent.setSelectedKey(aItems[iCP - 1].getKey());
}

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 O que não fazer em projetos de implantação SAP
entry.content:
Palestrante: Ely Barbosa
Biografia: Ely Barbosa é especialista em implantação de sistemas SAP, com vasta experiência em projetos de transformação digital e automação fiscal. Atua como consultor e líder de times em grandes corporações, especialmente no setor de energia e combustíveis.

Título da Palestra: O que não fazer em projetos de implantação SAP
Descrição: Nesta palestra, Ely Barbosa compartilha lições práticas e erros comuns em projetos de implantação SAP, especialmente nos módulos fiscais e de cálculo de impostos. Ele discute os desafios da integração com motores externos de cálculo tributário e o impacto das decisões de configuração no sucesso da operação.

    • Problemas com motores de cálculo externo
      Ely Barbosa apresenta casos de falhas em projetos SAP quando se confia cegamente em motores externos de cálculo de impostos. A responsabilidade dos cálculos permanece com o solicitante, e erros nos dados de entrada podem comprometer toda a operação. A validação prévia das informações e parametrizações é essencial para evitar erros fiscais.

    • Responsabilidade pelo cálculo tributário
      Mesmo com ferramentas automatizadas, o cálculo incorreto do ICMS continua sendo de responsabilidade do solicitante. O motor de cálculo apenas processa as informações recebidas, não atua como validador das regras fiscais. Isso exige do time técnico e funcional um controle rigoroso sobre cadastros e parametrizações.

    • Descontinuação de mapeamentos tradicionais
      Ely explica que com o uso de motores de cálculo externo, grupos de conditions e mapeamentos manuais, como as conditions BIC e BX, tornam-se obsoletos. O parceiro externo já retorna os resultados finais que são aplicados diretamente nas condições relevantes do SAP, eliminando partes significativas da configuração tradicional.

    • Integração com portais e e-commerces
      A palestra destaca que os motores de cálculo externo podem ser acionados tanto pelo SAP quanto por portais de vendas ou sites, desde que respeitem o padrão da API definida. Isso permite calcular preços finais com impostos embutidos com base em condições comerciais específicas como descontos.

    • Demonstração prática em ambiente SAP Fiori
      Ely finaliza a sessão com uma demonstração em tempo real usando SAP Fiori, mostrando como alterações simples em quantidades de pedidos afetam o valor do ICMS em tempo de execução. Essa flexibilidade e integração destacam os avanços do SAP S/4HANA em relação ao antigo ECC.

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Encerramento, Sorteio Final de Brindes & SAP Press
entry.content:
• Encerramento com agradecimentos e reflexões
    O encerramento do evento foi marcado por uma fala de reconhecimento à importância da comunicação nos projetos SAP e ao papel do gerente de projeto, mesmo sem conhecimento técnico aprofundado do ERP. A comunicação entre equipes, o entendimento dos riscos e a clareza sobre alocação de recursos foram destacados como fatores críticos para o sucesso de implementações.

  • Discussões sobre maturidade organizacional e desafios práticos
    Foi discutido como o grau de maturidade de uma organização pode impactar diretamente a execução e os resultados de um projeto SAP. Exemplos práticos mostraram falhas em infraestrutura, como falta de energia e falhas na contingência, revelando a importância de uma abordagem gerencial sólida e preventiva.

  • Início do sorteio com organização prática
    O sorteio de brindes e livros começou de forma descontraída, com interação entre os organizadores e o público. A dinâmica envolveu a conferência dos números distribuídos nos kits, e a promessa de que os sorteados seriam contatados posteriormente pela SAP Press para escolherem um e-book entre mais de 500 opções.

  • Informações logísticas sobre os prêmios
    Os prêmios sorteados incluíram livros digitais da SAP Press e brindes físicos. Foi utilizado um aplicativo para garantir sorteios aleatórios.

  • Clima descontraído e participação ativa do público
    O sorteio foi conduzido de maneira leve, com piadas e interações divertidas entre os participantes e os apresentadores. Algumas pessoas brincaram sobre não terem sido sorteadas, aumentando a leveza do encerramento do evento.

  • Transição para última palestra técnica
    Após o sorteio, iniciou-se a palestra de Renan Correia sobre DRC, destacando a complexidade dos projetos e lições práticas para evitar falhas, encerrando o evento com conteúdo técnico relevante.

 

Sorteio SAP Press

entry.title: 🚀 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 6 AI Agent を REST API 化
entry.content:
生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

お知らせ
You can find the English version  here.

 

📖 本シリーズで学べること

  • SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI Agent を “秒速” で動かす方法
  • LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAGを使った実装
  • AI Agent を REST API 化し、SAPUI5/Fiori の UI に載せ、Cloud Foundryにデプロイする手順

学習時間
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 プロローグ
  • Part 1 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG 基礎 ①: HANA Cloud VectorEngineと埋め込み処理
  • Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 AI Agent を REST API 化 [現在のブログ]
  • Part 7 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を構築
  • Part 8 CloudFoundry にデプロイ

注記
続編は順次公開予定です。

この記事がお役に立ったら、ぜひ Kudos を押していただけると励みになります。 「ここをもっと詳しく知りたい」「別のテーマも取り上げてほしい」など、ご要望があればお気軽にコメントください！

AI Agent を REST API 化

1｜はじめに

本章では、これまで Notebook 上で開発してきた AI Agent を REST API として公開し、後で Cloud Foundry（CF） にデプロイできる形にまとめます。API化 することで、以下のメリットがあります:

  • 再利用性：CLI や別のアプリケーション、SAP UI5 のフロントエンドなど、あらゆるクライアントから HTTPリクエスト で呼び出せる
  • スケーラビリティ：プロセスを Webサーバ に常駐させることで複数リクエストに対応できる
  • 運用性：CF上での自動スケールやログ管理、認証制御など、クラウド環境の標準的な仕組みを活用できる

本章では、FastAPI を用いて簡単な WebAPI を実装し、LangChain ベースの AI Agent をエンドポイント化します。

 

2 | 事前準備

  • BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

Trial 環境の注意
Trial の HANA Cloud インスタンスは 毎晩自動停止 されます。日をまたぐ作業の場合は翌日インスタンスを再起動してください。

 

3 | フォルダ構成とライブラリ準備

CF へデプロイすることを見据え、プロジェクト用のフォルダを新しく用意します。以降説明するコードや設定ファイルは、全てこのフォルダ内で管理します。

# フォルダ構成
my-ai-agent-api/
├── main.py
├── requirements.txt
├── .env
└── （デプロイに必要なファイルを追加予定）

 

CF にデプロイすることを見据えて requirements.txt で、必要な Python パッケージを明示しておきます。CF ではアプリケーション起動時、requirements.txt にしたがってライブラリを自動インストールします。今回新しく記載した fastapi や uvicorn、gunicorn は Webサーバ として必須のライブラリです。
以下にようにライブラリを列挙します。

# generative-ai-hub-sdk の公式ドキュメントで指定されている依存関係群
generative-ai-hub-sdk
ai_core_sdk>=2.5.7
pydantic==2.9.2
openai>=1.56.0
langchain~=0.3.0
langgraph==0.3.30
langchain-community~=0.3.0
langchain-openai>=0.2.14
langchain-google-vertexai==2.0.1
langchain-google-community==2.0.7
langchain-aws==0.2.9
google-cloud-aiplatform==1.61.0
boto3==1.35.76

# SAP HANA Cloud VectorSearch 用
hdbcli==2.24.24
langchain-hana==0.1.0

# .env ファイルを読み込むため
python-dotenv==1.1.0

# FastAPI／ASGI サーバー
fastapi==0.109.0
gunicorn
uvicorn[standard]==0.27.0

 

ローカルで動作確認するために、新たに仮想環境を用意しましょう。 Part 5 までに使用した仮想環境を流用しても構いませんが、ここでは念のため新規で作成します。

cd my-ai-agent-api
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt

 

同様に、環境変数ファイル（.env）も用意しましょう。

# SAP AI Core 認証情報
AICORE_CLIENT_ID = "<YOUR_AICORE_CLIENT_ID>"
AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET = "<YOUR_AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET>"
AICORE_AUTH_URL = "https://<your-region>.authentication.<your-region>.hana.ondemand.com"
AICORE_BASE_URL = "https://api.ai.prod.<your-region>.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com"
AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP = "<YOUR_RESOURCE_GROUP>"
DEVELOPMENT_ID = "<YOUR_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_ID>"

# HANA Cloud 接続情報
HANA_DB_ADDRESS = "<YOUR_HANA_DB_HOST>.hana.trial-<region>.hanacloud.ondemand.com"
HANA_DB_PORT = <YOUR_HANA_DB_PORT>          # 例: 443
HANA_DB_USER = "<YOUR_HANA_DB_USER>"
HANA_DB_PASSWORD = "<YOUR_HANA_DB_PASSWORD>"

# Google カスタム検索 API
GOOGLE_CSE_ID = "<YOUR_GOOGLE_CSE_ID>"
GOOGLE_API_KEY = "<YOUR_GOOGLE_API_KEY>"

これで、必要なパッケージのインストールと環境変数の用意が完了、API 開発の準備が整いました！

 

4 | main.py の作成

ここからは main.py ファイルを作成し、FastAPI を使って AI Agent を呼び出すエンドポイントを実装していきます。Part5 で使っていたノートブックのセルをほとんどそのまま組み合わせて作成しますが、AI Agent がどのように考え、何のアクションを取ったのかチャット画面で確認できるように、いくつか修正を加えます。
具体的には、以下の流れで構造化されたレスポンスを返すようにします:

  1. 環境変数の読み込み（.env）
  2. FastAPI アプリケーションの初期化
  3. LangChain / Google 検索・HANA Cloud Retriever Tool の作成
  4. AI Agent の初期化（AgentType を CHAT_ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION）
  5. ログから Thought / Action / Observation を取り出す関数の定義
  6. AI Agent からのレスポンスを変換
  7. /agent/chat エンドポイントの実装
import os
import re
import uvicorn
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# LangChain関連のインポート
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.tools import Tool
from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool
from langchain_google_community import GoogleSearchAPIWrapper
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

# HANA関連のインポート
from langchain_hana import HanaInternalEmbeddings
from langchain_hana import HanaDB
from hdbcli import dbapi

# 環境変数を読み込む
load_dotenv(verbose=True)

# FastAPIアプリケーションを初期化
app = FastAPI()

# CORS設定
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

# リクエストモデル
class QueryRequest(BaseModel):
    query: str

# ChatOpenAIを初期化
chat_llm = ChatOpenAI(deployment_id=os.getenv("DEVELOPMENT_ID"))

# Google検索ツールを設定
search = GoogleSearchAPIWrapper(k=5)
google_tool = Tool.from_function(
    name="google_search",
    description="Search Google and return the first results",
    func=search.run
)

# embeddingsを初期化
embeddings = HanaInternalEmbeddings(
    internal_embedding_model_id="SAP_NEB.20240715"
)

# HANA Cloudと接続
connection = dbapi.connect(
    address=os.getenv("HANA_DB_ADDRESS"),
    port=os.getenv("HANA_DB_PORT"),
    user=os.getenv("HANA_DB_USER"),
    password=os.getenv("HANA_DB_PASSWORD"),
    sslValidateCertificate=False,
    autocommit=True,
)

# データベースの立ち上げ
db = HanaDB(
    embedding=embeddings,
    connection=connection,
    table_name="TEST_TABLE"
)

# retrieverツールを設定
retriever_tool = create_retriever_tool(
    retriever=db.as_retriever(),
    name="hana_vectorengine",
    description=(
        "Use this tool to search internal SAP documents stored "
        "in HANA Cloud Vector Engine when the user asks company-specific questions."
    ),
)

# エージェントを初期化
agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.CHAT_ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION,
    verbose=True,
    return_intermediate_steps=True,
)

def extract_thought_from_log(log):
    if not log or not isinstance(log, str):
        return ""

    # 正規表現を使用して "Thought" 部分を抽出
    patterns = [
        r'Thought:\s*(.*?)(?:\nAction:|$)',
        r'^(.*?)(?:\nAction:|$)',
        # 日本語対応
        r'思考:\s*(.*?)(?:\nアクション:|$)',
        r'^(.*?)(?:\nアクション:|$)'
        ]

    # 複数のパターンを試行
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, log, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            thought = match.group(1).strip()
            # 空文字列や改行のみの場合はスキップ
            if thought and not thought.isspace():
                return thought

    # マッチしない場合は元のログを返す
    return log.strip()

def transform_response(raw_response):
    if not isinstance(raw_response, dict):
        return {"output": "", "intermediate_steps": []}

    output = raw_response.get('output', '')
    intermediate_steps = raw_response.get('intermediate_steps', [])

    if not isinstance(intermediate_steps, list):
        intermediate_steps = []

    structured_steps = []

    for idx, step in enumerate(intermediate_steps, 1):
        try:
            # ステップが辞書形式の場合（既に構造化されている場合）
            if isinstance(step, dict):
                structured_steps.append(step)
                continue

            # ステップがタプル形式の場合
            if isinstance(step, tuple) and len(step) == 2:
                action, observation = step

                # ログからThoughtを抽出
                thought = ""
                if hasattr(action, 'log') and action.log:
                    thought = extract_thought_from_log(action.log)

                # 構造化されたステップを作成
                structured_step = {
                    "step_no": idx,
                    "thought": thought,
                    "action": getattr(action, 'tool', 'unknown'),
                    "action_input": getattr(action, 'tool_input', {}),
                    "observation": observation if observation is not None else ""
                }
                structured_steps.append(structured_step)

        except Exception:
            # エラーが発生した場合はスキップ
            continue

    return {
        "output": output,
        "intermediate_steps": structured_steps
    }

@app.post("/agent/chat")
async def chat(request: QueryRequest):
    """エージェントにクエリを実行し、構造化されたレスポンスを返す"""
    # エージェントを実行
    raw_response = agent.invoke({"input": request.query})

    # レスポンスを変換
    structured_response = transform_response(raw_response)

    return structured_response

if __name__ == "__main__":
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)

このセルで何をしているか

  1. AgentType.CHAT_ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION: この設定を指定することで、AI Agent が「Thought → Action → Observation」という形で内部ステップを出力します。（ return_intermediate_steps=True を指定しないと response に含まれないので注意）
  2. extract_thought_from_log(): AI Agent の出力ログ（英語・日本語）から「Thought」の部分を抽出する、自作の正規表現関数です。
  3. transform_response(): (action, observation)形式のタプルを { step_no, thought, action, action_input, observation } という辞書形式に変換します。 

AgentType.CHAT_ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION の注意
このシリーズでは、チャットUI 上に AI Agent の思考プロセスを表示するため、AgentType.CHAT_ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION を採用します。ただし、この AgentType は "ツールを使わない質問" に対してエラーを返すことがあります。現在、より良い対処方法を検討中ですので、暫定的にこの点にご留意ください。

 

5 | 動作を確認する

それでは、用意した Python ファイルを実行し、作成した API が意図通りに動作することを確認しましょう。最初にローカル環境で FastAPI アプリケーションを起動し、API ドキュメント画面を見ながら実際にエンドポイントへリクエストを飛ばしてみます。その後、帰ってきた JSON が「output」と「intermediate_steps」の構造を満たしているかチェックします。

仮想環境に入っていることを確認しつつ、ターミナルで以下のコマンドを実行し、FastAPI アプリケーションをローカルサーバとして立ち上げます。

gunicorn -w 1 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000}

起動が完了すると、「Application startup complete」というログが表示されます。Business Application Studio（BAS）で実行している場合は以下のような画面が確認できます。

ポップアップで出てきた右下の「Open in a New Tab」を押し、URLに/docsを追加して下さい。 FastAPI が自動生成した Swagger UI が表示され、/agent/chat のエンドポイントに対してどのようなリクエストを投げられるか、インタラクティブに確認できます。

『POST - /agent/chat』のアコーディオンメニューを開き、「Try it out」をクリックします。Request ボディに JSON 形式で { "query": "任意の質問文" } を入力し、送信ボタンを押すと API が即座に応答します。

画面の最下部のように HTTPステータスコード"200" が返り、Response Body が「output」と「intermediate_steps」の構造を満たしていることを確認できたら成功です！

 

6｜チャレンジ – テキストファイルアップロード機能を追加しよう

ユーザが新しく用意したテキストファイルをアップロードし、HANA Cloud VectorEngine に格納されるエンドポイントを追加しましょう。この機能を用意しておくことで、ユーザはチャット画面からドキュメントを AI Agent に渡すことができます。

以下のコードを、main.py のインポート部やモデル定義、エンドポイント定義の適切な位置にまとめて挿入してみましょう。具体的な行番号やセクションはお任せしますが、インポートやモデルのそれぞれに対応するように挿入するか、あるいはファイルアップロード専用のブロックとしてまとめると良いかもしれませんね。

# ファイルアップロード用のインポート
import shutil
import tempfile
from fastapi import File, UploadFile, HTTPException
from langchain_community.document_loaders import TextLoader
from langchain.text_splitter import CharacterTextSplitter

# レスポンスモデル
class UploadResponse(BaseModel):
    message: str
    filename: str
    chunks_created: int

@app.post("/agent/upload")
async def upload_file(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
    """テキストファイルをアップロードしてHANAに埋め込みデータとして格納"""
    
    # ファイル形式のチェック
    if not file.filename.endswith('.txt'):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail="txt形式のファイルのみアップロード可能です。"
        )
    
    try:
        # 一時ファイルとして保存
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False, suffix='.txt') as tmp_file:
            shutil.copyfileobj(file.file, tmp_file)
            tmp_file_path = tmp_file.name
        
        # テキストファイルを読み込み
        text_documents = TextLoader(
            tmp_file_path
        ).load()
        
        # テキストを適切なチャンクに分割
        text_splitter = CharacterTextSplitter(
            chunk_size = 50,
            chunk_overlap = 0,
        )
        
        text_chunks = text_splitter.split_documents(text_documents)
        
        # HANAベクターデータベースに格納
        db.add_documents(text_chunks)
        
        # 一時ファイルを削除
        os.unlink(tmp_file_path)
        
        return UploadResponse(
            message="ファイルが正常にアップロードされ、埋め込み処理が完了しました。",
            filename=file.filename,
            chunks_created=len(text_chunks)
        )
        
    except Exception as e:        
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=500,
            detail=f"ファイル処理中にエラーが発生しました: {str(e)}"
        )

ポイントは、FastAPI で受け取った UploadFile をそのまま扱うのではなく、必ず tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile にコピーしてパスを取得し、そのパスを TextLoader に渡す点です。こうすることで、ファイルの中身を安全にローカルディスクに保存しつつ、その後チャンク分割してHANA Cloud に送ることができます。

コードを追加したら、先ほどと同様の手順で動作確認を行います。

 

アップロード後、HANA Cloud Database Explorer を開き、テーブル "TEST_TABLE" を確認します。アップロードしたテキストがチャンクに分割されて新規レコードとして追加されており、各レコードに対してベクトルが生成されているか確認します。

これらが正しく反映されていれば、ファイルアップロード機能の実装は完了です！

 

7｜次回予告

Part 7 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を構築

Part 7 では、これまでに作成した AI Agent API を呼び出すフロントエンドとして、SAPUI5 ベースのチャット UI を組み立てます。お楽しみに！

 

免責事項

本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: Re: Lead Time Options
entry.content:
Hello @Tim2015 

Unfortunately, you can't split delivery time into production, transportation etc. lead times. SAP ERP doesn't handle FOB-type scenarios nicely. That's why SAP has developed an EXE add on within SCM Consulting Solutions:

  • SAP Help: Ex Works External Supplier
  • OSS note: 2011146 - SCM CS: Optimization of scheduling for purchase orders - Ex Works External Supplier (EXE)
  • Knowledge Platform: Additional Materials

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: ICMR IC Matching run problem
entry.content:
Hi @NikitaDmitrenko,

In each matching run, the system processes all the relevant records (as long as they are in the method-level filters) based on all matching rules sequentially. It's not possible for the system to select partial records to run the matching. The system doesn't work that way.

Best regards,

Grace

entry.title: Improve BP master data with SAP MDG DQM to reduce superfluous costs in invoicing processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In invoicing processes with suppliers and customers, bank account information such as account number and IBAN play an important role. If this information is not correctly maintained in the business partner master data, costs arise from faulty bank transactions, and business partner relationships may also be strained due to delayed payment processes.

With the help of SAP MDG DQM validations and derivations, such situations can be avoided. A fully automatic reconciliation between account numbers and IBAN can be carried out. Initially, erroneous data records are detected through a DQM validation rule and a DQM evaluation run. Subsequently, these data records can be automatically corrected via an MDG mass process.

Background:

Depending on the configuration and history of the SAP S/4HANA system, account information and IBAN numbers are stored in different database tables. Account numbers and other details are found in the BUT0BK table. Associated IBAN numbers are stored in the TIBAN table. More detailed information on this is well described in the SAP Community Blog SAP S/4HANA Business Partner: Handling of IBAN with CVI.

When new business partners are created using SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP MDG, the information is correctly written into the tables, and no action is required. However, due to the migration of legacy data, interfaces, and data transfers, it can happen that the IBAN numbers, which contain the account number, do not match the dedicated account numbers in the BUT0BK table. In payment transactions, incorrect or improperly formatted account numbers are then used, and bank transactions can fail.

With the help of the SAP MDG DQM validation rule explained here, such erroneous BUT0BK entries are detected. The DQM rule will use a standard function module via BRF+ to calculate the correct bank account numbers from the IBAN numbers. A DQM evaluation run will iterate over all BUT0BK entries and identify erroneous records according to the rule. The user can then send these erroneous records to a mass process. This will automatically correct the account number in BUT0BK through a derivation step. Here, too, the SAP standard function module for calculation is reused.

DQM Validation Rule

The validation rule is created using the SAP MDG app "Validations Rules for Business Partners." The base table is the table BUT0BK, and the field being checked is BUT0BK-BANKN.

The following screenshot shows the rule in the app:

The scope of the rule defines which records are checked. It is important that only records with an IBAN in the bank details are checked. Therefore, the scope expression includes a BRF+ artifact to read the IBAN from TIBAN using the function module READ_IBAN_EXT through the parameters of the BUT0BK table. If an entry/IBAN is found, the record can be validated and is "in scope."

The following screenshot shows this scope expression:

The condition expression itself compares the value from BUT0BK-BANKN with the result of the function module CONVERT_IBAN_2_BANK_ACCOUNT. If the values are different, it means that there is an error for this data record. The following screenshot shows the condition expression.

 

 

 

DQM Derivation Scenario / Rule

 

The derivation rule is very similar to the validation rule and uses the same function blocks. Instead of detecting an error, a derivation can also write or correct the value.

In this case, an extra derivation scenario was defined for this demo in the SAP MDG DQM app "Define Derivation Scenario for Business Partners." The name is, for example, "Correct Bank Account Numbers." The base table is BUT0BK, as in this case the BANKN field of this table is corrected. The scenario only requires one rule at the field level. Additional rules are not necessary.

As with the validation rule, the scenario scope can also act on the presence of an IBAN for the bank account number. This ensures that only relevant records are processed in the derivation scenario.

The rule on the BANKN field is relatively simple to define: There are no condition fields, and only the BANKN field is defined as the result field. The screenshot shows the rule in the app "Define Derivation Scenario for Business Partners."

 

The decision table CALC_BANKN_FLD_DT generated by the SAP MDG framework also uses the SAP standard function modules to calculate the correct value for the bank account number via the IBAN. The following screenshot shows the details:

 

All DQM artifacts must be approved and activated after implementation. Additionally, the rule itself must be set to the status "Execution – YES".

Running the scenarios

 Execute Evaluation Run

If no automatic execution of the evaluation run is scheduled, it can be started via the SAP MDG app "Evaluate Data Quality."

 

 

Review of the evaluation run

With the app "Evaluation Results for Business Partners," the result can be examined in detail. By applying a filter to the rule, the erroneous data sets can be isolated and viewed. In the following screenshot, you can see that 7 business partners do not comply with the rule:

 

Remediation process with Mass Processing

To initiate a remediation process for one or more datasets, use the "Process" functionality directly from the "Evaluation Results for Business Partners" app.

In the following screenshot, 2 datasets are placed into a mass process.

 

In the following pop-up screen, the process template to be executed can be selected.

Info: This popup is only available in Cloud Ready Mode. In classic mode, the standard template is always used.

During the process, the derivation scenario is executed. The following screenshot shows the process after the derivation rule. It can be seen that the two incorrect bank accounts in BUT0BK have been corrected.

It is also apparent that a business partner has obviously defined multiple bank accounts, and one of them was already correct. Therefore, it was not overwritten.

Rerun the evaluation process.

If the evaluation run is triggered again, the data quality will have improved because the two corrected data sets will no longer be recognized as errors.

Compare the result with point 1: only 5 bank accounts are still incorrect.

Hope this MDG scenarios makes sense for you and please give feedback. In case of questions don’t hesitate to add them to this blog

Best Regards

Steffen

SAP Master Data Governance SAP Data Quality Management

entry.title: Re: Calling NW RFC SDK methods from go environment
entry.content:
• First of all: the NW RFC library doesn't have any methods. It's a plain C library, so it only exposes functions.
  • The functions you mention, return correct data when called from C. Therefore the conclusion is: the bug must be in the component that is responsible for the "Go <--> C interop". So you need to contact the team that is responsible for the Go <--> C interop (Go compiler team). We have no clue, how this works internally, so SAP can't help here.

entry.title: Managing Fixed Term Employees in SuccessFactors: Best Practices and Considerations
entry.content:
In today’s evolving talent marketplace, organizations increasingly rely on fixed term employees—professionals hired for a specific period—to drive projects, cover peak periods, or provide unique expertise. Managing these employees effectively requires robust HR technology. SAP SuccessFactors delivers the flexibility and features to support fixed term workforce needs, ensuring seamless processes from recruitment to offboarding. Fixed-term employees are valuable company resources who make significant seasonal contributions to the workforce. Proper handling of fixed-term employees ensures accurate workforce data and reporting. Correct management of fixed-term contracts is essential for legal and regulatory compliance. Fixed-term employment scenarios can impact various aspects of the SAP SuccessFactors system, including Employee Central, time management, and document generation. 

Here is an overview of how the various SAP SuccessFactors modules support the Fixed Term Employment.

Employee Central , the foundation of SuccessFactors, provides robust support for fixed term employees through:

  • Contract Management: You can define employment contract types (Permanent, Fixed Term, Intern, etc.) and record contract start/end dates during the hiring process or via job information updates.
  • Automated Alerts: Business rules and workflows allow you to set reminders for managers or HR when a fixed term contract is approaching its end, enabling timely discussions about renewal or offboarding.
  • Comprehensive Documentation: Attach legal documents such as contracts, amendments, or extensions to employee records, ensuring compliance and a complete audit trail.

Succession and Development: Decide whether to include fixed term staff in performance, succession, and learning programs via configurable business rules or eligibility settings.

Goal & Performance Management: Offer objective-driven review cycles tailored for fixed term workers, aligned to their contract duration and deliverables.

Learning Assignments: Assign compliance or job-specific training automatically, based on contract length, to ensure fixed term employees remain compliant and productive.

Recruitment: Create job requisitions specifying both the fixed term contract type and anticipated duration. Applicant communications can clarify the nature and expectations of such roles.

Onboarding:  Develop onboarding experiences tailored to fixed term hires, highlighting documentation, induction, and provisioning tasks specific to contract-based employment.

Organizations can optimize their fixed term employee management through the following enhancement opportunities:

  • Introduce automation for contract extension offers, acceptances, and employee record updates, making renewal processes efficient and consistent.
  • Build dashboards that visualize contract status, remaining time, and highlight upcoming actions, providing HR and managers with actionable insights.
  • Set business rules that automatically update employees’ eligibility for talent programs, depending on contract duration or proximity to contract end.
  • Enhance the offboarding process with checklists and workflows uniquely designed for fixed term employees, ensuring smooth exits and compliance with company procedures.

When managing fixed-term employees in SAP SuccessFactors, there are several important considerations to keep in mind. Legal compliance is crucial, so configure Employment Information settings to align with local labor regulations for fixed-term contracts, including limits on renewals and triggers for conversion to permanent roles. Ensure fair inclusion of fixed-term staff in performance assessments, learning opportunities, and rewards programs to make them feel valued and supported. Protect their contract and personal data in accordance with applicable data protection standards, especially in jurisdictions with stringent privacy rules.
Utilize automated reminders and clear checklists to ensure smooth contract completions, covering tasks like equipment return, system access removal, and exit surveys. Maintain open communication about contract terms, renewal processes, and expectations throughout the employee lifecycle, from recruitment through offboarding. 
By addressing these considerations, organizations can effectively manage fixed-term employees while ensuring compliance, fairness, and positive employee experiences.

Fixed-term employment scenarios often receive insufficient attention during User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and integration testing phases. This oversight can lead to unexpected issues and data discrepancies in the production environment. It's crucial to treat fixed-term employment scenarios with the same level of scrutiny as other employment types for several reasons.
Incorporating fixed-term scenarios in testing helps maintain consistency across all employment types and ensures smooth transitions between different employment statuses.
On including comprehensive fixed-term employee scenarios in UATs and integration tests, organizations can preemptively address potential issues, ensuring data integrity and system reliability across all employment types.

Challenges foster innovation
While SuccessFactors provides a solid foundation, several areas present opportunities for enhancement:

  • Contract End Management: There’s an opportunity to further streamline contract completion and renewals.
    Currently, mass handling of contract ends (batch renewals or offboarding triggers) may need manual monitoring or bespoke workflows.
  • Reporting Flexibility: Out-of-the-box analytics may not always allow detailed filtering by employment type, so organizations may benefit from tailored reports or custom data fields to view fixed term employment specifics.
  • Succession Planning Inclusion: At times, fixed term employees may not be included in succession pipelines by default, even if their skills or potential for extension would warrant consideration.
  • Rehire Process Consistency: On re-hiring a former fixed term employee for a new contract, maintaining accurate service records and recognizing previous contributions might require additional configuration.

Fixed term employees are a vital part of a flexible and resilient workforce strategy. SAP SuccessFactors, when strategically configured, can empower HR teams to manage the entire fixed term employee lifecycle efficiently and compliantly. By focusing on key process areas, leveraging system enhancements, and respecting unique compliance and cultural needs, organizations can maximize value for both the business and fixed term staff.

How have you used SuccessFactors to support your fixed term employees? Share your insights, tips, or questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Alerting for availability status of a secondary db maintained in DBCO table
entry.content:
Hi,

Regarding to setting up 'SAP NETWEAVER ABAP DBCON' connection method (SAP note 1601608) in a SAP system with ABAP stack, DBCON method only supports a connection to external databases whose edition is 'Microsoft SQL Server 'Enterprise' Edition.
This support statement information is indicated in "SAP NetWeaver/Microsoft SQL Server" installation guides and some SAP notes, e.g. note 62988, note xxxxxx Release planning for Microsoft SQL Server 20xy.
The DBSL library for Microsoft SQL Server database, included in SAP Kernel (SAPEXEDB package - 'LIB_DBSL dbmssslib.so component), was only developed for 'Microsoft SQL Server 'Enterprise' Edition.

SAP NetWeaver ABAP systems DBCON method only supports connection to following database types:

DB2 DB2 UDB for OS/390
DB4 DB2 UDB for AS/400
DB6 DB2 UDB for Unix and Windows
MSS Microsoft SQL Server 'Enterprise Edition'
ORA Oracle
HDB SAP HANA
SYB SAP SyBase ASE

Regarding to SAP note 3210043, this note introduces a workaround for/it is only meant to be applied for setting up DBCON to 'Microsoft SQL Server "Standard" edition"'. However, Note 3210043 is to be considered only as a temporary workaround while the SAP customers prepare to upgrade the Microsoft SQL Server "Standard Edition" to "Enterprise Edition".
SAP cannot support a product not being tested thoroughly, such as the SQL Server "Standard" Edition.

Other SQL Server editions such as Express, Developer, Web, etc .. are not supported by SAP NETWEAVER ABAP DBCON connection method.

Regards,
Riccardo

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI MFA with Microsoft Entra (Part I): Integration with SAP Secure Login Service
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

check out this blog: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/bridging-identity-worlds-real-world-look-sap-cloud-services-olt-kzpwe/

Cheers Colt

Info from SAP for this kind of configuration: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-different-trust-configurations-for-same-identity-authentication?q=Corporate%20Identity%20Provider

Example: Each SAP app (e.g., BTP subaccounts, SAC, ALM) is mapped 1:1 to an Enterprise App in Entra ID. This enables fine-grained control: per-app CA policies, scoped token claims, dedicated audit trails, and the ability to separate internal, external, and privileged access paths. 

 

Why does this matter? Take SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as an example. One global Enterprise App for all use cases might sound convenient – until you realize that marketing wants external agency partners to access specific dashboards, while controlling strictly requires MFA and device compliance for their own analytics. By isolating SAC in its own Enterprise App, we could apply different policies and user groups without compromising security or creating exceptions elsewhere. The same logic applied to BTP: separating productive, test, and sandbox environments at the identity level allowed us to enforce stricter rules for critical apps while keeping development agile.

entry.title: Re: Does role template apply to created POD?
entry.content:
Hi,

"Default" PODs are available by default to all personas ootb.

Custom PODs created in POD Designer are not assigned to users via roles. Each individual user can "Publish" individual PODs from POD Designer app, or PODs can be assigned to multiple users by administrator via Manage POD user app, so the PODs will be available for the assigned users as part of Launch POD app.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Vendor Refund for AP Down Payment – Best Practice in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are looking for guidance on how to handle the following scenario in SAP Business One:

An AP Down Payment Invoice was processed, and an Outgoing Payment was made to the vendor. Later, the vendor refunded the amount (either partially or fully).

As the down payment is already closed due to the outgoing payment, we are unable to generate an AP Credit Memo against it. This is blocking us from properly reflecting the vendor refund in the system.

What is the recommended way to handle such a vendor refund in SAP Business One?

Any guidance or shared experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Can the billing block be removed from the billing pla...
entry.content:
Hi,

moval for Sales Orders in Down Payment Processing process required for a sales order. When changes such as removing a billing block are made and the sales order is saved, Master Data - Billing Block it often triggers a need for re-approval. This ensures that all modifications reflect the correct validations and authorizations as per the organization’s workflow configurations, maintaining system integrity and compliance.

If you have configured your system to require approval after removing billing blocks or if your workflow specifically mandates re-approval for any changes made to the sales orders, then re-approval will be necessary.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Generate dynamic file name from Email content
entry.content:
Hi Helmut, Thank you for your valuable input,
my experience with Java is not professional

However I tried to replicate your code according to my expectations

My expectation is, the java code  it parse the subject of email by using header parameter write in the code (SHeaderSUBJECT) and assign to dynamic parameter


And Example of Subject format is : Contract 111111/2abce9953
and expectation of file name should be at the end :  Contract_2abce9953

public class setFilename extends AbstractTransformation {

private static final DynamicConfigurationKey KEY_FILENAME  = DynamicConfigurationKey.create("http://sap.com/xi/XI/System/Mail", "SHeaderSUBJECT");

public void execute(InputStream in, OutputStream out, DynamicConfiguration conf, InputHeader header, InputParameters inputParam, InputAttachments inputAtt, OutputAttachments outputAtt) throws StreamTransformationException {

Iterator itKeys = conf.getKeys();
while (itKeys.hasNext()){
DynamicConfigurationKey key = (DynamicConfigurationKey) itKeys.next();
String valueOld = conf.get(key);
String valueNew = StringEscapeUtils.escapeXml10(valueOld);
conf.put(key, valueNew);
}
                conf.put (KEY_FILENAME.substring(16,26))
             


out = in;

}
​after substring of the value, how to concatenate the additional text to after the substring value (Example of output : Contract_2abce9953)?
Thank you
Sateesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m Vitaly, originally from Germany, and I’ve been working in the SAP logistics field for over a decade. A while ago, I made the move to beautiful Cyprus — and I couldn’t be happier with the combination of sun, nature, and digital opportunities this island offers.

I’m especially excited to connect with others from Cyprus (or those who are planning to move here). I believe in strong communities and knowledge-sharing, so I’ve started a local SAP Community group for Cyprus. https://community.sap.com/t5/cyprus/gh-p/cyprus

Whether you're new here or have been around for a while, I’d love to connect, meet up in person, discuss SAP-related topics, and support each other in solving real-world problems.

If you're in Cyprus or feel addressed by this — feel free to reach out or join the group. Let’s make the SAP Community here even stronger!

Looking forward to hearing from you — where are you tuning in from?

Best regards from sunny Cyprus,
Vitaly

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Testing with SAP Cloud ALM - Overview
entry.content:
Thank you for this very detailed blog! 😊

I was wondering if you happen to know whether it's possible to create a test case directly from a requirement. I'm aware that we can assign existing test cases to requirements, but I'm specifically looking to create a new test case in direct relation to a requirement.

The reason for this is that some scope items, such as 5HG, do not have process diagrams. As a result, it's not possible to create a manual test case for these scope items in SAP Cloud ALM. Instead of creating a self-authored process diagram—which is quite time-consuming and likely unnecessary, as we expect SAP to provide a standard best practices diagram—we’re exploring whether it’s possible to create a test case directly linked to a requirement, to which the scope item (without a process diagram) would be assigned.

Thanks in advance for any insights you can share! 

Leslie

entry.title: Re: CCAP_ECN_MAINTAIN use
entry.content:
Use class  /plmi/cl_ecn_bo

 /plmi/cl_ecn_bo=>get_instance( IMPORTING eo_bo = go_ecn ).

    iv_change_no = <fs_data>-aennr.

    go_ecn->get_ecn(
     EXPORTING
       iv_change_no     = iv_change_no
       iv_refresh       = iv_refresh
       iv_lock_flag     = iv_lock_flag
       iv_include_saved = iv_include_saved
     IMPORTING
       es_ecn           = es_ecn
       et_message       = et_message
       ev_severity      = ev_severity
          ).

 

  go_ecn->lock(  EXPORTING
                    iv_change_no = iv_change_no
                   IMPORTING
                    et_message   = et_message
                    ev_severity  = ev_severity ).

 

**  do your thing e.g. change date

 go_ecn->shift_valid_from_date(
      EXPORTING
        iv_change_no      = iv_change_no
        iv_valid_from_new = iv_valid_from_new
      IMPORTING
        et_message        = et_message
        ev_severity       = ev_severity
           ).

 go_ecn->check_before_save(
      IMPORTING
        et_message  = et_message
        ev_severity = ev_severity
           ).

    go_ecn->save(
      IMPORTING
        et_message        = et_message
        ev_severity       = ev_severity
           ).

    go_ecn->unlock( iv_change_no ).


    COMMIT WORK.

entry.title: Re: Planning based on batch expiry date - Shelf life planning in SAP S4 HANA PPDS
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu
